summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Student Fee Status Report feature with multi-select Classes & Sections, removed Min/Max Due inputs, and PDF/XLSX/CSV export functionality. All backend APIs (39/39 tests) and frontend features passed successfully.

backend_issues.notes: All backend endpoints working perfectly. No issues found.

frontend_issues.notes: All frontend features working correctly. Multi-select, filters, downloads, and UI elements all functional.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/reports/fee-status returns 200 with correct response structure
Backend: min_due and max_due params are properly ignored (removed feature)
Backend: class_sections param works with single class:section format
Backend: class_sections param works with class:blank (all sections) format
Backend: class_sections param works with multiple pairs (OR semantics)
Backend: Backwards compatibility - old class_id + section params still work
Backend: status_filter param works for paid/partial/unpaid
Backend: PDF export returns valid PDF with correct Content-Type
Backend: XLSX export returns valid Excel file with Fee Status and Summary sheets
Backend: CSV export returns valid CSV with correct headers
Backend: All exports respect class_sections and status_filter params
Backend: No regression on existing endpoints (collection.*, analytics/fees)
Frontend: Multi-select button for Classes & Sections present (data-testid='report-classes-multiselect')
Frontend: Min Due / Max Due inputs correctly REMOVED
Frontend: Popover opens with class list and search input
Frontend: Class master checkbox toggles all sections
Frontend: Individual section chips are toggleable
Frontend: Selection chips appear with X remove buttons
Frontend: 'Showing:' label displays correct text (e.g., 'Class I (A) · Class II (A)')
Frontend: Apply button calls API with correct class_sections param
Frontend: KPI cards update correctly (Students, Expected, Paid, Due, Paid/Partial/Unpaid)
Frontend: Clear all button clears all selections
Frontend: Multiple selections work (OR semantics) - 22 students for Class I-A + Class II-A
Frontend: Search input filters table rows correctly
Frontend: Status filter works (All, Paid, Partial, Unpaid)
Frontend: PDF download button triggers download/opens in new tab
Frontend: XLSX download button triggers file download
Frontend: CSV download button triggers file download (toast notification visible)
Frontend: Fee Collection tab still works (no regression)
Frontend: Table displays filtered results correctly

backend_highlights: All 39 backend tests passed with 100% success rate
class_sections parameter correctly handles multiple formats: 'class_id:section', 'class_id:', 'class_id:section,class_id:section'
OR semantics working correctly - multiple class:section pairs return union of students
Backwards compatibility maintained - old class_id + section params still work
All three export formats (PDF, XLSX, CSV) working with correct Content-Types and valid file formats
Exports correctly respect filters (class_sections and status_filter)
No regression on existing endpoints (collection reports, analytics)
frontend_highlights: Multi-select popover UI is intuitive and functional
Class master checkboxes correctly select/deselect all sections
Individual section chips are independently toggleable
Selection chips display with X buttons for easy removal
'Showing:' label correctly displays selected classes/sections (e.g., 'Class I (A) · Class II (A)')
KPI cards update dynamically based on filters (verified with multiple test cases)
Search functionality filters table rows in real-time
Status filter correctly filters by paid/partial/unpaid status
Download buttons all functional - CSV download toast notification visible in screenshot
Fee Collection tab unchanged and working (no regression)
Min Due / Max Due inputs successfully removed from UI
test_scenarios_verified: Single class with specific section (Class I - A): 14 students
Single class with all sections (Class I): 22 students
Multiple class:section pairs (Class I-A + Class II-A): 22 students
Clear all selections: reverts to all 102 students
Class III - A selection: 8 students (all unpaid)
